title: Superiority To Fate.
content:
Superiority to fate
    Is difficult to learn.
    'T is not conferred by any,
    But possible to earn

    A pittance at a time,
    Until, to her surprise,
    The soul with strict economy
    Subsists till Paradise.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Superiority To Fate.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
